_meta.description: Hard-OOC queries (M3 Phase 5 OOC threshold tuning). Questions a user might plausibly ask Tarvis that are semantically distant from the portfolio corpus (weather, sports, recipes, celebrity trivia). Distinct from absent-facts.json, whose entries are semantically adjacent to the corpus (in-domain but factually absent — e.g. 'Tushar's Rust experience' pulls experience.md chunks at cosine 0.37–0.47 even though Rust isn't mentioned). These hard-OOC queries target top-1 cosines <0.30 against any source-scoped search, giving the guardrail-firing-rate metric a population that can actually move when the cosine floor is tuned. Both files keep the `out-of-corpus` tag so they contribute to the same firing-rate aggregate; the writeup at docs/eval/ooc-threshold-tuning.md explains the soft-vs-hard distinction and the rationale for the chosen floor.

scoring.out-of-corpus: correct_chunks is empty; success = zero chunks above the current cosine floor (see DEFAULT_COSINE_FLOOR in scripts/eval/dispatch.ts)

queries.query: What's the weather like in Tokyo this week?
Will it rain tomorrow in Mumbai?
Who won the cricket world cup final?
How do I make carbonara from scratch?
